topic: project-memory-hook-detection
finding: mkdir flagged as research tool because isExploratoryBash() in pre-tool-use.js uses two-layer detection: Layer 1 checks description keywords (exploration vs operational), Layer 2 checks command regex. If description scoring is ambiguous or missing, the fallback may misclassify operational commands.

topic: hook-intent-fix
finding: Fixed both hooks: post-tool-use.js now has isExploratoryBash() and isExploratoryTask() to skip save reminders for operational tools. pre-tool-use.js GATE 2 now uses isExploratoryTask() so operational Task subagents (code-simplifier, gsd-executor) skip memory check and escalation gates.

topic: Hook intent detection: false positive root cause and fix for Bash/Task classification
finding: post-tool-use.js was flagging ALL Bash commands (mkdir, npm install, git push) as research tools because it never called isExploratoryBash() — the function only existed in pre-tool-use.js. Similarly, ALL Task subagent uses were unconditionally hard-blocked via IMMEDIATE_SAVE_TOOLS set. Fix: (1) Added isExploratoryBash() with EXPLORATION_KEYWORDS/OPERATIONAL_KEYWORDS scoring + EXPLORATION_PATTERNS regex fallback to post-tool-use.js, with early exit for non-exploratory Bash after periodic summary checkpoint. (2) Added isExploratoryTask() checking subagent_type against EXPLORATION_SUBAGENTS set (Explore, Plan, general-purpose, feature-dev:code-explorer, feature-dev:code-architect) to both hooks. (3) Updated pre-tool-use.js GATE 2 isExploratory check from 'input.tool_name \!== Bash' to a 3-way ternary: Bash uses isExploratoryBash(), Task uses isExploratoryTask(), WebSearch/WebFetch always exploratory.

topic: project-memory plugin hook architecture: pre-tool-use and post-tool-use gating system
finding: The project-memory plugin uses two complementary hooks in plugins/cache/.../hooks/scripts/. MATCHED_TOOLS={Bash, WebFetch, WebSearch, Task} gates both hooks. Pre-tool-use.js has 3 gates: GATE 1 blocks exploratory Task subagents (EXPLORATION_SUBAGENTS set) until check-memory.js runs. GATE 2 blocks exploratory tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, exploratory Bash via isExploratoryBash(), exploratory Task via isExploratoryTask()) until memory is checked — non-exploratory Bash/Task pass through. GATE 3 denies all exploratory tools when reminderCount exceeds ESCALATION_THRESHOLD (2) and no save detected. Post-tool-use.js: IMMEDIATE_SAVE_TOOLS={Task, WebSearch, WebFetch} trigger hard block with immediate save demand (only for exploratory Task subagents after fix). Non-exploratory Bash and non-exploratory Task exit early with {}. Exploratory Bash uses gradual escalation (gentle systemMessage nudge for first 2 reminders, then hard block) throttled to 3min intervals. State persisted in .ai-memory/.last-reminder as JSON. Save detection via mtime of decisions.jsonl/research.jsonl. Self-calls (save-decision, save-research, check-memory, session-summary) always pass through both hooks.

topic: FlaUI DOM dump format and UI tree inspection capabilities
finding: FlaUI does NOT produce XML DOM dumps natively like Appium. Instead: (1) FlaUI treats the UI automation element tree as an XML-like structure for XPath navigation — control types become element names, automation properties become attributes. (2) The Debug class (FlaUI.Core.Debug) provides a Details() method that creates a comprehensive text report of an element and its entire subtree using caching. (3) FlaUInspect tool can visualize the tree with XPath display. (4) Programmatic access via FindAllDescendants()/FindAllChildren() walks the tree but returns AutomationElement objects, not XML strings. (5) The robotframework-flaui library adds XPath-based element location on top of FlaUI. To get an XML-like dump comparable to Appium page source, you would need to manually serialize the AutomationElement tree — walking descendants and outputting properties (AutomationId, Name, ControlType, ClassName, BoundingRectangle, IsEnabled) as XML attributes. FlaUI does not have a built-in GetPageSource() equivalent.

topic: Windows verification pipeline: XML-hardcoded, FlaUI JSON not directly usable
finding: The verification pipeline (DomService.cs, ActionEffectVerifier.cs, MobileTestScriptExecutorService.cs) is XML-hardcoded throughout: DomService.ExecuteVerificationQueries() uses XPathDocument+XPath evaluation (line 217), ComputeDomDiff() uses XmlDocument (line 100), FetchDomXmlAsync() expects XML declaration markers. VerificationDetail model has XmlQuery field assuming XPath syntax. The LLM prompt (AndroidTestVerificationPrompt.cs) generates XPath queries like //node[@class='android.widget.Button']. FlaUI's FindAllDescendants() returns AutomationElement[] (C# objects, not XML/JSON). The WindowsMcpServer's find_element in FlaUIToolHandler.cs returns TEXT format only (line 349-358), not structured data. ElementPropertyAccess.cs exposes: Name, AutomationId, ClassName, ControlType, IsEnabled, IsVisible, BoundingRectangle, etc. To enable Windows verification: (1) add ElementTreeSerializer in WindowsMcpServer to serialize full tree as JSON, (2) add get_full_element_tree MCP tool, (3) extend IDomService/DomService with JSON parsing path or convert JSON to XML, (4) create WindowsTestVerificationPrompt with JSONPath or XPath-on-converted-XML examples.

topic: AIAssistedTestAutomation local tool vs MCP tool architecture for Windows page source
finding: Android/iOS uses LocalTool pattern: methods decorated with [LocalToolAttribute] in AppiumLocalToolsService.cs are discovered via reflection at startup (IGUIInteractionService.DiscoverLocalTools scans assemblies for LocalToolAttribute). GetPageSourceAsync (line 249-257) returns Task<ToolResult> with XML in Message field prefixed with 'Page source:\n'. InvokeLocalToolAsync (line 125-313) handles reflection-based invocation. Windows uses MCP-only via WindowsInteractionService which delegates to WindowsMcpClient.CallToolAsync (JSON-RPC). WindowsInteractionService does NOT support LocalTool type. To add GetPageSource as local tool for Windows: (1) create WindowsLocalToolsService with [LocalToolAttribute(platforms: WindowsDesktop)] method, (2) add LocalTool support to WindowsInteractionService.CallToolAsync, (3) or alternatively add get_page_source as MCP tool in FlaUIToolHandler.cs. Both verification disabled in Mobile (commented out TODO) and Windows (returns Passed unconditionally). Mobile has ExecuteVerificationStepAsync fully implemented but disabled, Windows lacks the method entirely.

topic: Replaced check-memory.js substring search with LLM-as-reader approach
finding: Rewrote check-memory.js to dump ALL entries instead of filtering with keyword scoring. Claude (the LLM already running in the session) reads all entries and performs semantic evaluation natively. No API key, no SDK, no npm dependencies needed. The script outputs a SEMANTIC EVALUATION REQUIRED header instructing Claude to match on synonyms, abbreviations, conceptual overlap. Each entry shows full finding/decision text. Closing directive tells Claude to USE relevant findings and cite them. Stats still recorded, .last-memory-check still written to unlock PreToolUse gates.

topic: Added mandatory Memory Hit banner when Claude finds relevant entries from check-memory
finding: Updated check-memory.js closing directive and session-start.js CITE MEMORY instruction to include a mandatory green banner template. When Claude finds relevant entries from memory, it MUST display: ★ Memory Hit banner showing matched topics and estimated savings (~1000 tokens, ~2 min per finding). The banner uses the same ★ Insight format for consistency. This is enforced in two places: (1) check-memory.js output that Claude reads after each memory check, (2) session-start.js system message that Claude receives at session start.

topic: BM25 text search is implementable in pure JavaScript
finding: BM25 (Best Matching 25) can be implemented in ~50-80 lines of pure JavaScript with zero npm dependencies. Core algorithm: (1) build inverted index mapping terms to document IDs, (2) compute IDF = log((N - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1) where N=total docs and df=docs containing term, (3) for each query term compute score = IDF * (tf * (k1+1)) / (tf + k1*(1-b+b*dl/avgdl)) where k1=1.2, b=0.75, tf=term frequency, dl=doc length, avgdl=average doc length. No tokenizer library needed — splitting on whitespace + lowercasing is sufficient for memory search. Packages like 'wink-bm25-text-search' exist but add unnecessary dependencies.

topic: project-memory shared.js DRY module extracts duplicated utilities
finding: Created scripts/shared.js extracting findProjectRoot, readJsonl, appendJsonl, tokenize, entity index CRUD, breadcrumb logging, BM25 search, and dedup checking. Previously duplicated across save-research.js, check-memory.js, session-summary.js, save-decision.js.

topic: save-research.js enhanced with --entities, --related flags and dedup warning
finding: save-research.js now accepts --entities 'A,B' and --related 'id1,id2' CLI flags. Entities are stored lowercase in entry and indexed in entity-index.json for O(1) lookup. Dedup check warns (but does not block) if similar topic + 2 matching tags found. Backward compatible — saves without flags get entities:[] and related_to:[].

topic: check-memory.js large-store mode uses BM25 ranking + entity index instead of keyword filter
finding: Replaced keywordPreFilter() in check-memory.js with BM25 scoring (shared.buildBM25Index + bm25Score) merged with entity index lookup. Large store mode (>50 entries) now ranks candidates by BM25 relevance score and shows score in output. Entity index provides O(1) lookup for file/class/method names from query tokens.

topic: post-tool-use.js logs exploration breadcrumbs for unsaved detection
finding: post-tool-use.js now calls logExplorationBreadcrumb() for every exploratory tool use, appending {ts, tool, subagent, prompt/query/url, saved:false} to .exploration-log. session-summary.js reads this log via shared.getUnsavedBreadcrumbs() and shows UNSAVED EXPLORATIONS warning as hard block. session-start.js clears the log each session.

topic: session-start.js updated save instructions require atomic fact decomposition
finding: Save instructions in session-start.js and sync-tools.js (CLAUDE.md auto-save section) now explicitly require ATOMIC FACTS: each save should be ONE searchable fact (1-2 sentences), never bundled. Includes --entities guidance for better search indexing. BAD/GOOD examples provided in the instruction text.

topic: ONNX Runtime Node.js Embeddings
finding: onnxruntime-node v1.24.2 is available on npm (MIT license, 220MB unpacked). It's the official ONNX Runtime Node.js binding from Microsoft. Depends on: adm-zip, global-agent, onnxruntime-common. Suitable for CPU-only inference on Windows and Linux.

topic: @xenova/transformers for Node.js Embeddings
finding: @xenova/transformers v2.17.2 is a popular JavaScript implementation of Hugging Face transformers using ONNX Runtime. It provides feature-extraction pipeline for generating embeddings. Uses onnxruntime-web v1.14.0, sharp, and @huggingface/jinja. Works in Node.js and browsers. Supports MiniLM and other sentence embedding models.

topic: @huggingface/transformers vs @xenova/transformers
finding: @huggingface/transformers v3.8.1 (newer fork by Hugging Face) uses both onnxruntime-node v1.21.0 AND onnxruntime-web v1.22.0-dev. @xenova/transformers v2.17.2 (older, by xenova) uses only onnxruntime-web v1.14.0. Hugging Face version has better Node.js CPU optimization with native onnxruntime-node.

topic: Transformers.js feature-extraction pipeline for embeddings
finding: Transformers.js provides 'feature-extraction' pipeline task that generates embeddings from text. Equivalent to Python Hugging Face pipeline('feature-extraction'). Returns numerical vector representation of input text. Requires specifying model ID (e.g., 'Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2' for sentence embeddings). No separate MiniLM-specific package needed—all models are loaded via ONNX from Hugging Face Hub.

topic: Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 ONNX model availability
finding: Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 is the ONNX-converted version of sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Available on Hugging Face Hub. Can be loaded directly in transformers.js without explicit download. Model size varies (quantized ~22MB, full ~45MB). No separate model package needed on npm—models are fetched on-demand from Hugging Face CDN or cached locally. Works CPU-only, no GPU required.

topic: Dashboard upgraded to global aggregation across all projects
finding: Dashboard now discovers all .ai-memory directories under USERPROFILE (up to 3 levels deep), aggregates research/decisions/embeddings/stats across all projects. Each entry tagged with _project name. Projects tab shows per-project breakdown. build-embeddings.js supports --all flag for global embedding.
